summary: First-run E2E testing of Adspora completed. Backend: 24/24 pytest tests passed across auth (login/demo/me/logout), dashboard (overview/trends/platform-breakdown), creatives (list/filter/search/detail), AI (SSE streaming responds), upload (CSV success + invalid format), alerts (list/dismiss+verify), settings (GET/PUT+verify). Frontend: All 13 critical flows passed - login page, admin login (admin@adspora.ai/admin123), demo login, dashboard (6 stat cards with real data, 3+ charts), creatives page (24+ rows, search works, row click navigates to detail), AI Studio loads, Upload loads with dropzone, Alerts page loads, Settings page loads, sidebar navigation works, logout works. No critical issues found.

minor.issue: Uses bson.ObjectId(creative_id) without try/except - invalid (non-24-char) IDs raise InvalidId -> 500 instead of 404. Same applies to /api/alerts/{id}/dismiss and /api/ai/diagnose. Tested with valid 24-char fake ID which returns 404 correctly, but malformed IDs would crash.

issues: Dual Y-axis scaling appears off: Revenue line sits flat near zero while right axis goes up to 26000. Data is rendered but lines visually overlap/cluster at bottom of chart - hard to read trend. Consider using separate charts or log scale.

action_items: (Minor) Wrap ObjectId(...) calls in try/except InvalidId -> raise 404 in /api/creatives/{id}, /api/alerts/{id}/dismiss, /api/ai/diagnose, /api/ai/generate to avoid 500 errors on malformed IDs.
(Cosmetic) Improve ROAS & Revenue Trend dual-axis scaling on dashboard so both series are visually readable.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 581 lines - within acceptable limits but approaching the 700-line split threshold; consider splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/dashboard.py, routers/creatives.py, services/seed.py for future maintainability.
CORS uses allow_origins=[FRONTEND_URL, 'http://localhost:3000'] with allow_credentials=True - correctly explicit (not wildcard with credentials). Good.
JWT cookies set with httponly=True, samesite='lax', secure=False. secure=False is acceptable for preview env but should be True in production over HTTPS.
bcrypt password hashing used correctly ($2b$ format via gensalt).
seed_admin is idempotent and updates password if changed - good.
No rate limiting/brute force lockout implemented on /api/auth/login. Not strictly required by playbook for MVP but worth noting.
predict_days_remaining uses random.randint at seed-time only (persisted) - acceptable since it's stored, not recomputed on read.
AI streaming endpoints correctly use SSE (text/event-stream) with X-Accel-Buffering: no. Confirmed first chunk arrives within 30s.
test_credentials: admin@adspora.ai / admin123 (verified working). Demo button auto-creates demo@adspora.ai.
seed_data_creation: App auto-seeded 24 creatives across Meta/Google/TikTok/Taboola + 6 alerts on startup. Upload test added 1 TEST_Upload_Ad creative (count now 25).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_adspora.py runs in ~5s with pytest-xdist (2 workers). All endpoints, auth (cookies+JWT bearer), and SSE streaming verified. Frontend data-testids all present and working. Only minor non-blocking items: ObjectId 500 on malformed IDs, and dual-axis ROAS/Revenue chart scaling. AI diagnose endpoint streams via SSE - confirmed responsive with valid first chunk within timeout.
rca of the issue: No critical issues to RCA. App is in solid working state on first-run test.
